sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient, Brandon, presents with neck pain, which began after a car crash in February of this year.
He reports experiencing multiple car crashes, with the most recent one being in February of this year.
He describes his pain as sharp and incapacitating, rating it a 12 out of 10.
He also reports experiencing headaches, dizziness, numbness in his left arm and right leg, and spasms throughout his body.
He has been experiencing fatigue since the accident.
He has been prescribed fentanyl in the past but has not received a new prescription in several weeks.
He has seen a therapist and a pain clinic, and his mother and a lawyer have advised him to seek medical attention.
Past medical history: [NOT MENTIONED]
Review of systems: The patient reports headaches, dizziness, numbness in his left arm and right leg, and spasms throughout his body.
Current medications: [NOT MENTIONED]
Objective:
Vital signs: [NOT MENTIONED]
Physical exam: The patient's neck pain is exacerbated by palpation on the bony process and muscle.
He has a moderate range of movement in his neck, but it hurts to bend forward and backward.
There is no bruising, swelling, or laceration visible.
Test Results: The patient had an x-ray prior to this visit, which did not show a fracture.
The doctor plans to order an MRI to get a more thorough image of the area.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has a neck sprain.
Problem list: Neck sprain; suspected fracture (status: suspected).
Plan:
I prescribed Robaxin 1500 mg every 6-8 hours as needed for muscle relaxation and pain relief.
I also advised the patient to apply ice to the affected area, perform exercises to maintain range of motion, and referred him to physical therapy.
The patient will be referred to pain medication and local injections if necessary.
The patient will be given a note excusing him from work until the MRI results are available.
Follow-up: The patient will return for an MRI and a follow-up appointment to discuss the results and further treatment.
